title: [gengar] Battle Guide
summary: A classic [ghost]/[poison] special sweeper with 130 SpA and 110 Speed. [nasty-plot] sets up sweeps that bypass most of the metagame defensive core.
strengths: [nasty-plot] doubles SpA for guaranteed OHKO ranges
[cursed-body] can disable opposing contact moves randomly
weaknesses: 60/60/75 bulk is fragile against neutral hits
Priority attackers shut down setup attempts

recommendedMoves.reason: Fighting coverage for Dark, Steel, Normal walls

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[gengar] is a [ghost]/[poison] special sweeper that has been a metagame staple for years thanks to its combination of 130 SpA and 110 Speed. Its only ability is [cursed-body], which disables opposing contact moves 30% of the time — a minor but occasionally match-winning utility. In Champions, it serves as a dedicated fast wallbreaker that sets up with [nasty-plot] and closes with [ghost]/[poison] STAB.

## Strengths
Speed 110 sits above most of the upper metagame, so [gengar] rarely loses the race unboosted. [nasty-plot] doubles SpA, turning [shadow-ball] and [sludge-wave] into guaranteed OHKO moves against most frail and mid-bulk targets. The [ghost]/[poison] STAB combination covers [fairy], Grass, and Fighting cleanly, and [cursed-body] provides passive defensive value against contact attackers.

## Weaknesses & Answers
60/60/75 bulk is fragile — any neutral priority or 2x coverage move threatens a KO. Dark, [ghost], Ground, and psychic are all 2x weaknesses and common in the metagame. Priority moves are the biggest threat because they bypass the Speed advantage entirely, which means [focus-sash] is the default item choice. Scarfed attackers above 110 also force a Speed-tier recheck.

## Recommended Moveset
[shadow-ball] is the locked [ghost] STAB for consistent damage. [sludge-wave] is the [poison] STAB finisher with the highest base power available. [focus-blast] handles Dark, Steel, and Normal walls that would resist both STABs. The last slot is [nasty-plot], the doubles-SpA setup move that unlocks the sweep win condition.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default because the first setup turn needs protection against priority. [life-orb] is the pure damage alternative that trades survival for higher OHKO ranges. [choice-scarf] is the Speed-tier reshape that drops setup for revenge utility. [cursed-body] is the only ability so there is no choice. Tera [ghost] maxes [shadow-ball] damage, and Tera [fairy] flips Dark and Dragon weaknesses.

## Teammates & Synergy
[gengar] pairs with fake-out leads and pivots that generate safe setup turns. [fairy] or Steel partners cover the Dark and psychic weaknesses, and a Flying partner handles the Ground weakness. A cleaner behind [gengar] converts chip damage into final KOs, especially on frail cores that already absorbed a setup turn.

## Play Patterns
Find a free turn to [nasty-plot], then open with [shadow-ball] on neutral matchups and [sludge-wave] on Fairies. Use [focus-blast] on Dark or Steel switch-ins, and Tera on any telegraphed Dark or psychic move. Save [focus-sash] for the priority threat.
